name: Law 29.733 on the Protection of Personal Data
summary: Peru's data protection law was enacted in 2011 with implementing regulations effective May 2013. A major update via Supreme Decree 016-2024-JUS took effect March 2025, modernizing obligations including extraterritorial application and local representative requirements for foreign entities.
keyRequirements: Obtain free, prior, express, informed, and unequivocal consent
Register personal data banks with the national registry
Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures
Respect data subject rights (access, rectification, cancellation, opposition)
Restrict cross-border transfers to countries with adequate protection or with consent
Appoint a local representative for foreign entities
Comply with purpose limitation, proportionality, and data quality principles
penalties: Minor infractions: up to 5 UIT (~USD 5,500). Serious infractions: up to 50 UIT (~USD 55,000). Very serious infractions: up to 100 UIT (~USD 110,000). Enforced by the Autoridad Nacional de Proteccion de Datos Personales (ANPDP) within the Ministry of Justice.
